question: What is the minimal detectable difference when using $n=30$ per group in the cholesterol illustrative example? Assume $\alpha=0.05$ (two-sided) and power $=0.90$. We have established 0.25 as a reasonable estimate for $\sigma^2$.
answer: $\Delta=\sqrt{ rac{2 \cdot\sigma^2}{n}}\left(z_{1- eta}+z_{1- rac{\alpha}{2}}ight)=\sqrt{ rac{2 \cdot 0.25}{30}}(1.282+1.96)=$ 0.42 . This study will be able to detect a mean difference of $0.42 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$ under the stated conditions.
